chunk:
CHUNK:009/021
\u201cYes, yes.\u201d The man nodded. \u201cThe very same. We came on the cruise ship, you know.\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. \u201cDreamcaster.\u201d \u201cThe very same.\u201d The man smiled and then turned to the woman. \u201cJoyce, didn\u2019t you say you saw the local police arrive onboard shortly before we left on this excursion?\u201d \u201cI did,\u201d Joyce said. She gave me a stern look, and I guessed that I was unlikely to win her over, and if Atii had been there, it would have been up to her to charm the older woman into thinking I wasn\u2019t really as rude as she might think. But, as I reminded myself, the clock was ticking. I had the diary, and it was time to go. \u201cYou\u2019ve been very kind,\u201d I said, pressing my palm on the cover of the diary and raising it slightly as if the pages contained weighty evidence that would make or break a case. \u201cThis will really help.\u201d \u201cI hope so,\u201d Joyce said. \u201cBut don\u2019t expect an easy read. Never mind that it\u2019s in another language. The writer has scribbled all kinds of notes in the margins, and even between the lines. And then there\u2019s the mould,\u201d she said. \u201cI recommend gloves, Officer, unless you want to catch something unpleasant.\u201d I didn\u2019t, and I thanked her for the tip before excusing myself and stepping outside. I opened the diary to check it really was Paniula\u2019s, and then, seeing my name on the first page, I closed it just as quickly and slipped it into one of the larger pockets in my jacket. I checked the time on my smartphone and estimated that \u2013 even with the wind and waves against me \u2013 I could still get back to the ship in time to take a quick shower and maybe even borrow a shirt from the crew. I pressed my hand to my pocket and clutched the diary through the fabric. And then, taking a breath, I started to run. \n 7 I kept the diary dry inside my pocket for the choppy trip back to the cruise ship. The wind had shifted, and gave me a helpful shove from behind, giving me the chance to make up for lost time. I passed several Zodiac boats powering towards Qassiarsuk to pick up the passengers in stages and waved at each of the boat drivers on the way. The crew on duty guided me in alongside the floating dock with unnecessary but well-meaning gestures until they had secured the dinghy, and I turned the motor off. They mentioned something about a prolonged stay and wondered if they could secure the dinghy together with the Zodiacs when they removed the floating dock, and I nodded that they could. I didn\u2019t think Gaba would object, and the casual mention of a prolonged stay suggested the crew were preparing to loiter in Qinngua for a little while longer. \u201cBut could I borrow a fresh shirt?\u201d I asked, before climbing the ladder to the middle deck. \u201cAsk in security,\u201d the crewman said, and I nodded that I would. I found our cabin, showered, and changed, and reported to Gaba wearing a fancy \u2013 and fitted \u2013 dark grey Dreamcaster security t-shirt that did far more for my figure than our standard uniform shirt ever would \u2013 and fixed Gaba with my most winning smile. \u201cYou didn\u2019t sleep, did you?\u201d he said, shaking his head as he returned my ballistic vest and waited for me to put it on. \u201cI\u2019m rested,\u201d I said, tightening the hook and loop straps of the vest with a surprisingly satisfying shrickk when I adjusted them. What I actually meant was I was wired. Paniula\u2019s diary was quite literally burning a hole in the cargo pocket of my trousers, and I knew that dipping into it would keep me awake better than caffeine. Although I didn\u2019t say no when Gaba said there was fresh coffee on the table opposite the cabins. I turned around to see Saarullik and Taateraaq rising from what looked like comfortable seats in an alcove with a bay window, one of many dotted along the length of the corridor. They looked bored, and I sent them on their way with an enthusiastic smile that only made them shake their heads, as if they knew that an hour of babysitting would knock any remaining enthusiasm right out of me. But they didn\u2019t have Paniula\u2019s diary, I thought, convincing myself it was the secret to staying awake while babysitting the American diamond smugglers. \u201cThere\u2019s a rumour going around,\u201d Gaba said as he pressed my MP5 into my hands. He waited for me to adjust the sling, so the weapon was cinched comfortably in front of my chest. It was lighter than I remembered, but Gaba continued before I could query it. \u201cSomething about guests discovering jewellery in their pockets.\u201d Gaba jerked a stiff thumb at the door to Arnet\u2019s cabin and said, \u201cI\u2019m guessing he\u2019s up to something, but the Americans have made it clear Arnet is a US citizen, on a US registered ship, and we are not allowed to talk to him.\u201d \u201cNot allowed?\u201d I said, as Gaba nodded at the table. I followed him the few metres across the corridor and greedily reached for a sandwich when I spotted one. Gaba gave me the kind of look Atii did when she was convinced I was hiding something and then poured fresh coffee into two paper cups. \u201cThanks,\u201d I said, wiping crumbs and mustard sauce from my mouth with the back of my hand before taking a cup from Gaba\u2019s hand. \u201cWhat\u2019s going on, Constable?\u201d he said, keeping it professional as I stuffed the last of the sandwich into my mouth and eyed the second. Gaba followed my gaze and nodded for me to take it. \u201cIs this what happens when you catch up on a little Greenland culture?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said, between bites and slurps of coffee. \u201cAlways.\u201d \u201cRight,\u201d Gaba said.
compressed_context:
CTX: QASSIARSUK DOCK
MAN: ARRIVED ON CRUISE-SHIP DREAMCASTER
JOYCE: LOCAL-POLICE ARRIVED BEFORE EXCURSION
OFFICER RECEIVES PANIULA'S-DIARY
JOYCE WARN: DIARY=FOREIGN-LANG ∧ MARGIN-NOTES ∧ MOULD ∴ RECOMMEND: GLOVES
OFFICER EXITS; RUNS BACK TO SHIP ∧ WIND SHOVE BEHIND
DOCKING: ZODIAC-PASS ∧ CREW SECURE-DINGHY ∧ DISCUSS PROLONGED-STAY
OFFICER BORROWS SHIRT AT SECURITY
OFFICER CHANGES → WEARS DARK-GREY DREAMCASTER SECURITY-T-SHIRT
GABA RETURNS: HANDS BACK BALLISTIC-VEST ∧ GIVES MP5 TO OFFICER
OFFICER ADJUSTS MP5 SLING
OBSERVE: SAARULLIK∧TAATERAAQ BORE IN ALCOVE ∧ DISMISSED BY OFFICER
GABA STATES: RUMOR=GUESTS FIND JEWELLERY IN POCKETS
GABA: ARNET=US-CITIZEN ON US-REGISTERED SHIP ∧ TALK←FORBIDDEN
CREW SERVE COFFEE+SANDWICH
OFFICER EATS ∧ DRINKS
GABA: "WHAT’S GOING ON, CONSTABLE?"
OFFICER: "JUST CATCHING UP ON GREENLAND-CULTURE"
